hints.junior: If users can't bookmark or share a filtered view, the filter state is in the wrong place. Move it to the URL querystring.
hints.mid: URLSearchParams + history.pushState is all you need for URL state management; no router library required for the basic pattern.
hints.staff: URL filter state is SEO state. For e-commerce, category+sort URLs are indexable and can rank. Component-state filters are invisible to search engines.

hints.junior: Apply filters in broken mode, reload the page: they're gone. Fixed mode: apply filters, reload, they're back. The URL is the persistence layer.
hints.mid: Copy the URL from the fixed demo after applying filters and paste into a new tab. The filters are applied from the URL before any React state is set.
hints.staff: The popstate event is the back/forward hook. Without it, browser navigation doesn't sync the URL state back to your UI. Always handle it in URL state patterns.

hints.junior: If a user can't bookmark or share a filtered view and get the same results back, the filter state is living in the wrong place. The URL is the fix, not component state.
hints.mid: React Router's useSearchParams handles the URL-to-state sync natively — you don't need a manual URLSearchParams + pushState dance once you're inside a router that already ships this.
hints.staff: Filter state in Redux when it belongs in the URL is a specific, common over-engineering trap: it adds store complexity while still failing to be shareable, since you'd need a URL sync layer on top of the store anyway to get the thing you actually wanted.

hints.junior: Don't forget the popstate listener — without it, clicking browser back or forward changes the URL but your UI just sits there unchanged, out of sync with what the address bar says.
hints.mid: Always merge into the existing search params — prev.set(key, val), never build a fresh URLSearchParams from scratch — or every other active filter gets silently wiped the moment one changes.
hints.staff: Build a typed URL-state hook once and reuse it everywhere, rather than letting each feature hand-roll its own URLSearchParams logic. The state-architecture matrix — path for identity, query for filters, component for ephemeral, store for cross-component, localStorage for persisted prefs — is the reference for where new state should actually live.

anecdotes.junior: An e-commerce client's filters were in Redux. Users couldn't share filtered lists. Migrating to URL params took one sprint and immediately increased filter-page conversion.
anecdotes.mid: The Redux-for-filters pattern is common but wrong. Redux is for server cache and cross-component state; URL querystring is for navigation state.
anecdotes.staff: For complex filter schemas (multi-select, date ranges), consider a URL serialization layer (nuqs, use-query-params) to avoid manual URLSearchParams manipulation.

sections.junior: Hey! Today we're fixing filters that disappear on page refresh. The solution is putting filter state in the URL: it's bookmarkable, shareable, and just works.
sections.mid: This case covers URL as state (URLSearchParams, history.pushState, popstate, and the React Router useSearchParams pattern).
sections.staff: We're covering the full state placement matrix, URL state for SEO implications, and when to use URL vs component vs global store for different state types.

sections.junior: The concept chapter is the principle: read it before the demo so the toggle makes sense.
sections.mid: Connect the principle to your component API: what prop or CSS change encodes the fix?
sections.staff: Map the pattern to observability: what would you measure in RUM or lab to prove the fix?

sections.junior: Apply filters in broken mode, reload the page: they're gone. Fixed mode: apply filters, reload, they're back. The URL is the persistence layer.
sections.mid: Copy the URL from the fixed demo after applying filters and paste into a new tab. The filters are applied from the URL before any React state is set.
sections.staff: The popstate event is the back/forward hook. Without it, browser navigation doesn't sync the URL state back to your UI. Always handle it in URL state patterns.

sections.junior: This section covers takeaway for url-as-source-of-truth.
